mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the cut is sustained rather than a one-off move, because persistent easing changes expected policy paths and equity valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in CPIAUCSL raises the probability of restrictive policy, compresses equity valuation multiples, and lowers S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. The effect is negative only when inflation is running hot enough to alter policy expectations, not when CPI is stable or decelerating.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 3.5% and FEDFUNDS is not falling
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce lower median 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered observations, or if the sign of the effect turns positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE marks weakening income growth and deteriorating demand, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is a short-horizon risk-off indicator because labor deterioration typically precedes lower earnings expectations and weaker cyclical equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly increases in UNRATE are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the relationship weakens to near zero in any 4-quarter rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE confirms labor-market broadening, which supports earnings expectations and risk appetite. That combination should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, while isolated moves in either series are not sufficient.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered episodes fail to deliver positive excess 20-day returns relative to the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS signals resilient household demand and revenue momentum for S&P 500 firms, supporting positive forward returns over a one-quarter horizon. The effect should be most visible when sales acceleration reflects real demand rather than inflation alone.
failure_condition: Reject if high RSAFS growth does not precede above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign is negative in more than half of high-sales-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and cyclical momentum, which boosts earnings expectations for large-cap equities over the next quarter. The signal is positive because production gains imply stronger throughput and better operating leverage for firms tied to the domestic cycle.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly INDPRO gains are not associated with higher 60-day forward SPX returns than monthly declines, or if the signal loses predictive power in consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals housing demand weakness and tighter cyclical activity, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal becomes active only when the housing decline is large enough to indicate broad demand softening rather than noise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of lower CPIAUCSL inflation and lower FEDFUNDS rate supports equity multiples by easing real-rate pressure and reducing recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This is an interaction effect because the benefit is strongest when inflation cools at the same time policy stays accommodative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation falls below 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered days do not show positive 5-day forward SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either single-series condition.
